raw.population: Matsuo: N:216 Age:33 (18-63) WT:- Messenheimer e.a.: N: 85 Age: 35 (18-64) WT:- Schachter e.a.: N:314 Age:35 (18-64) WT:-
raw.objective: “to explore the population pharmacokinetics of lamotrigine under clinical conditions of use and to validate the results of initial formal pharmacokinetic studies” and “to investigate the factors that may result in variability in lamotrigine pharmacokinetics”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, HT, AGE, SEX, race, duration of treatment, concomitant antiepileptic medication, and use of oral contraceptives Included: WT, RACE, comedication, SEX on CL
raw.observations: Variance of CL after inclusion of covariates was relatively low, which should result in accurate model-based dosing guidance, although visually comparing observed vs predicted concentrations for base and final model does not reveal significant differences.
raw.dose: Steady-state and non-steady-state oral doses in patients
raw.structure: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F=(theta1+theta2*WT)*(1+theta3*RACE)*(1+theta4*NAED)*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta5*(1+theta6*(1-SEX))*(1+eta2) Ka=theta7*(1+eta3) F=1+theta8*TAB Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) RACE=ethnicity (1=Caucasian, 0=non-caucasian) NAED=categorical; number of concomitant AEDs out of: CBZ, PHT, PRM, PHB SEX=gender (1=male,0=female), converted from (1=female,0=male) TAB=Tablet formulation (1=yes, 0=no)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=2.43 theta2=0.0257 theta3=-0.254 theta4=0.131 theta5=132 theta6=-0.265 theta7=1.30 theta8=0.91 omega1= 0.104 omega2= 0.196 omega3= 0.601 sigma1= 0.0538

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
